article_meta.title: Business Climate change study that overestimated economic impact is pulled — but not before banks relied on it By Taylor Herzlich Published Dec

reasoning: The summary is largely neutral and factual. It avoids the article’s more loaded framing and sensational tone, though it uses one mildly interpretive term (“dramatically overestimated”). It omits some perspectives (NGFS statement and authors’ framing that impacts remain substantial), but does not systematically adopt the source’s rhetoric.

custom_reasoning.spin: Mostly neutral verbs; “dramatically overestimated” adds mild interpretive spin.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All claims align with article details (retraction, 62% vs 23%, central banks’ use) and are supported.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Minimal; characterization of the overestimate is supported by figures.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No alarmist tone; language remains measured.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No attributions of motives or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes retraction and errors; downplays authors’ argument that impacts remain substantial and NGFS’s update stance.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits NGFS statement and authors’ policy-oriented framing that damages remain high.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Uses “dramatically” once; otherwise neutral descriptors or ones taken from the article (e.g., “substantial”).
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Focuses on the error/retraction but consistent with article’s main thrust.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No preferential credibility assigned to elites or grassroots.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the source’s framing; avoids “catastrophic,” “faulty number,” etc.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Key perspectives (NGFS, authors’ mitigation case) are missing, and sources_represented lists outlets rather than perspectives.
